tier_notes.nmf: 15 categories. Designed to surface as recognizable signals inside NMF topic clusters. Semantically concentrated — presence should shift what a topic means.
tier_notes.analysis: 48 categories. Designed for post-hoc cross-tabulation and framing analysis. May be distributed across many topics; not expected to dominate any single cluster.

injection_note: Both tiers are injected identically in 01.5. The tier distinction affects interpretation only — nmf-tier tokens in NMF top_terms are first-class topic signals; analysis-tier tokens are framing overlays.

field_notes.rationale: Human-maintenance field. Not passed to any LLM prompt. Explains design rationale.
field_notes.target_coverage_pct: Documentation target. Actual coverage is checked in 01.5 Cell 12 against enrichment.coverage_deviation_warn_threshold.
field_notes.counter_examples: Passed to the classifier prompt. Most important disambiguation signal per category — keep maintained.
field_notes.contrast_with: Documentation field. Not passed to LLM prompts currently.

schema_note: framing_taxonomy.json is a plain JSON array of category objects. Do not add top-level keys — this would break all load sites that iterate the array directly.
